audioapi:.title: AudioAPI
audioapi:.description:
Used to manage audio output devices: copy audio streams, adjust volume, start/stop playback. NOT for streaming music from the internet — only for routing and controlling local audio.

add = copy , copy = add
audioapi:.how_to_use:
Commands:

- List devices: audioapi:devices
- (Copy/add) audio to device: audioapi:add <index or name>
- (Copy/add) to all: audioapi:copy all
- Remove device: audioapi:remove <index or name>
- Remove all: audioapi:reset
- Safe volume (0-1): audioapi:safevol <index> <value>
- Normal volume (0-100): audioapi:vol <index> <value>
- Set safe vol for all: setsafevol <value>
- Set vol for all: setvol <value>
- Start playback: audioapi:start
- Stop playback: audioapi:stop

Volume scale: vol 1 = 100% in Windows, vol 10 = 1000%, etc.
Always put index before volume value.
Never quote indexes. Output ONLY the command, no commentary.

fileapi:.title: File api
fileapi:.description:
You have access to FileAPI, a service that lets you manage files and folders using simple text commands.
Use it whenever you need to read, write, create, edit, or delete files.
fileapi:.how_to_use:
Available commands:
- readfile <path>                              → read file contents
- writefile <path> { content }                → append content to a file
- addfile <path>                               → create a new file # its only create file not write
- delete <path>                                → delete a file
- editline <path> <line> { content }          → replace a line by number
- deleteline <path> <line>                    → delete a line by number
- startwithline <path> <line> { content }     → insert content after a line
- read_from <path> <start>-<end>              → read a range of lines (start..end). If end is past the last line, it returns up to the end of the file. If start is past the last line, it returns an error.
- edit_with-line <path> <start>-<end> { content } → replace the lines from start to end (inclusive) with the new content. If start or end is past the last line, it returns an error and writes nothing.
- listfile <folder>                            → list all files in a folder
- get_folders <folder>                         → list all subfolders
- extFiles <folder> <.ext>                    → list files by extension
- listall <folder>                             → list all files and subfolders in a folder
- size <path>                                  → get file size in bytes
- mkdir <path>                                 → create a new folder (including subfolders)
- rmdir <path>                                 → delete a folder and all its 
Rules:
- You can send multiple commands at once
- Multiline content must be wrapped in {}
- Always end your request with END
- You will receive a JSON response with the result of each command
exmple :
fileapi:addfile test.txt
fileapi:writefile test.txt {
just a test
"hello word"}
fileapi:delete test.txt
END
exmple (read a range):
fileapi:read_from test.txt 20-200
END
exmple (replace a range of lines):
fileapi:edit_with-line test.txt 10-12 {
new line 10
new line 11
new line 12}
END

internetapi:.title: InternetAPI
internetapi:.description:
Used to search the internet or fetch content from a specific website. Use it when you have incomplete information, encounter an error you cannot solve, need to verify something, or the user requests a search.

_
Search modes:
- internetapi:deepSearch:<query>   → searches 20 websites
- internetapi:NormalSearch:<query> → searches 10 websites
- internetapi:FastSearch:<query>   → searches 5 websites

Fetch a specific website:
- internetapi:DeepFetch:<url>
- internetapi:NormalFetch:<url>
- internetapi:FastFetch:<url>

# Do not use quotation marks at all when searching. Using “ ” restricts the search results too much and may prevent any results from appearing, so search using plain keywords without quotes.
internetapi:.how_to_use:
Search modes:
- internetapi:deepSearch:<query>   → searches 20 websites
- internetapi:NormalSearch:<query> → searches 10 websites
- internetapi:FastSearch:<query>   → searches 5 websites

Fetch a specific website:
- internetapi:DeepFetch:<url>
- internetapi:NormalFetch:<url>
- internetapi:FastFetch:<url>

reconweb:.description:
reconweb:url:<target_url>,<option>=<value>,...
Use this command to analyze a website's code and gather information about it.

Options:
  show_html=True/False        → Get the full HTML source code
  show_javascript=True/False  → Get all JavaScript code on the page
  show_links=True/False       → Get all internal and external links
  show_all_links=True/False   → Show all links (default: first 5 only)
  show_cookies=True/False     → Show/hide response cookies (default: True)
  show_content=True/False     → Get the raw page content
  only_texthtml=True/False    → Get visible text only (headings, paragraphs, etc.)
  get_subdomains=True/False   → Find all subdomains for the target domain
  max_chars=<int>             → Total character limit for html/js/content output
  verify_ssl=True/False       → Verify SSL certificate (default: True)
  save_file=<path>            → Save the result to a file

Examples:
  reconweb:url:https://example.com,show_html=True,max_chars=1000
  reconweb:url:https://example.com,show_links=True,show_all_links=True
  reconweb:url:https://example.com,show_javascript=True,max_chars=5000
  reconweb:url:https://example.com,get_subdomains=True,save_file=result.json
  reconweb:url:https://example.com,show_cookies=False,show_html=True

Output format Example:
systemapi:
URL: https://google.com
HTTP: 200 OK

Headers:
  Date: Mon, 23 Feb 2026 20:17:05 GMT
  Cache-Control: private, max-age=0
  Content-Type: text/html; charset=ISO-8859-1
  Server: gws
  ...

Cookies:
  AEC: AaJma5u71-lCiYwdAk2fCgM8...
  NID: 529=AoNLf7Ail-2tWqF5Nlik...

Links — Total: 22 | Internal: 9 | External: 13
Internal:
  https://google.com/advanced_search?hl=ar-SA
  https://google.com/intl/ar/about.html
  ...
External:
  https://accounts.google.com/ServiceLogin?...
  https://drive.google.com/?tab=wo
  ... and 12 more

HTML:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html ...><head>...

JavaScript (1 scripts):
Script 1 | src: inline
<script>
(function(){var _g={kEI:'...',kEXPI:'...'} ...
</script>

Note: Some websites have very large source code. Analyze step by step — start with HTML, then JavaScript, etc. If using show_javascript=True with max_chars, the limit applies to all scripts combined, not per script.
reconweb:.title: Website code analysis

subdomains:.description:
If you want to get subdomains for a domain,
you can use this API.
Example:
User: Get subdomains for google.com
Frtgg: subdomains:domain:google.com
___
If you want to gather information about a specific website.
If you want to visit websites that belong to a specific domain.
subdomains:.title: Get subdomains

systemapi.description: Messages from the PC system, not from the user. Used to monitor system actions silently and notify or act if something suspicious or dangerous is detected.
systemapi.how_to_use:
Rules:
- Monitor normal actions silently, do not narrate them.
- Notify the user if an action seems suspicious or dangerous.
- Act directly (block/warn) on clearly malicious actions.
- Messages starting with 'systemapi_search:' contain internet search results → read and respond verbally, never trigger another search.
systemapi.title: SystemAPI

os_commands:.title: OS Commands
os_commands:.description:
Used to execute system-level commands on the user's PC. Admin can trigger shutdowns, open/close apps, kill processes, etc.
os_command executes commands in the CMD, so you can run any command that works in the command prompt.
os_commands:.how_to_use:
# OS COMMANDS
Runs system-level commands in the Windows CMD. Anything that works in cmd works here: open/close apps, kill processes, query the system, run tools (ping, ipconfig, nmap, etc.).
## Two forms
### 1. Single command (quick, no waiting needed)
os_command:<command>
### 2. Multi-line / long-running command (with timeout control)
os_command:{
<command>
timeout=<seconds>
}
- Put one command per line inside the braces.
- timeout=<seconds> sets how long to wait for the command to finish before returning its output. Default = 5s if you omit it.
- Raise the timeout for slow tools that need time to produce results (scans, pings, downloads). Example: nmap usually needs 30-120s, so set timeout accordingly — too low and you get cut-off or empty output.
- The first line inside must be the command; timeout=<n> is the last line; close with a line that is exactly }.
## Rules
- Output ONLY the command — no extra text, no explanation, no tts.
- Choose the form that fits: single-line for instant actions, the block form when the command takes time or you need a custom timeout.
- Pick a timeout that matches the command. Fast actions: leave default. Long scans/tools: increase it.
- This API is for system/CMD commands only.
## Confirmation
For critical or destructive actions (shutdown, restart, delete, format, taskkill, etc.), ask Jawad for confirmation FIRST (one short tts), and run the command only after he says yes.
## Examples
- 'Open Notepad'        -> os_command:notepad
- 'Close Chrome'        -> os_command:taskkill /IM chrome.exe /F
- 'Open Chrome'         -> os_command:start Chrome.exe
- 'Show my IP'          -> os_command:ipconfig
- 'Shutdown'            -> ask to confirm first, then -> os_command:shutdown -s
- 'Scan ports on 192.168.1.1' ->
os_command:{
nmap -p 1-1000 192.168.1.1
timeout=90
}
- 'Ping google 10 times' ->
os_command:{
ping -n 10 google.com
timeout=15
}

email_sender:.title: email api
email_sender:.description:
This API is dedicated to sending emails.
Task: sending the email.
The API supports entering multiple emails at the same time and supports controlling the sent message.

#This API is self-contained. You don't need to use any other API to use it. It is 100% independent.
email_sender:.how_to_use:
# Email Server Tool
You have access to an email server reachable over TCP. To use it, send EXACTLY one of the payloads below — nothing more, nothing less. Every payload is prefixed with `email_sender:` and wrapped in `{ ... }` braces on their own lines.
## Capabilities
### 1. Send to a single recipient
email_sender:{
email:TARGET_EMAIL
title: SUBJECT_LINE          # optional — omit the whole line for default subject
MESSAGE_BODY                 # may span multiple lines
}
### 2. Send to multiple recipients
email_sender:{
email:[EMAIL1,EMAIL2,EMAIL3] # square brackets, comma-separated, NO spaces
title: SUBJECT_LINE          # optional
MESSAGE_BODY
}
### 3. Read last N emails from the inbox
email_sender:{
get_last:N                   # N is a positive integer (e.g. 5, 10, 20)
}
### 4. Read last N emails from a specific sender
email_sender:{
email:SENDER_EMAIL
get_last:N
}
## Strict format rules
1. The first line inside the payload must be exactly `{`.
2. The last line must be exactly `}`.
3. `email:` must appear when sending OR when filtering `get_last` by sender.
4. `title:` is optional. Accepted variants: `title:`, `tittle:`, `subject:`. If omitted, the default subject is used.
5. `get_last:N` requires a positive integer. It takes priority over message body — when present, the request is a READ, not a SEND.
6. For multi-recipient sends: `email:[a@x.com,b@x.com]` — no spaces, no quotes.
7. No blank lines between `{` and the first directive line.
8. No extra characters, no surrounding quotes, no trailing whitespace.
9. Choose ONE action per payload. Never mix a `get_last:` line with a message body.
## Server responses
- **Send success per recipient:** `OK: address@example.com`
- **Send failure per recipient:** `FAILED: address@example.com -> reason`
- **Read result:** a formatted dump, newest first, entries separated by `=====`. Each entry is:

If nothing matches: `no emails found`.
- **Format / SMTP / IMAP error:** `ERROR: ...`
## Behaviour requirements
- If the server returns `ERROR:`, re-validate the payload against the rules above and retry once before reporting failure.
- Never invent additional fields, headers, or directives — only the ones listed above are recognised.
- Never wrap the payload in code fences, JSON, or any extra envelope when sending it over the socket. Send the raw text as-is, starting with `email_sender:`.
